bullets.text: Thirteen people, including four women and a child, were killed in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon amidst ongoing fighting between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah, despite a ceasefire.
Cuba has condemned new US sanctions as 'illegal' and 'abusive,' which follow a US blockade of oil to Cuba causing widespread blackouts and fuel shortages.

The world's largest conference on human rights and technology, RightsCon 2026, has been cancelled by the Zambian government just days before its start, citing that it did not 'align with national values.'
Three people have died in an attack at a home in Rosemeadow, Sydney, leading to the arrest of a 32-year-old man.
Republicans in the US have expressed concerns regarding NATO alliances and security following the Pentagon's announcement of withdrawing 5,000 troops from Germany.
Farmers in Western Australia's food bowl region claim that levees built to prevent flooding actually worsened the situation during Cyclone Narelle.
The US is fast-tracking $8.6 billion in arms sales to Middle East allies to shore up regional partners during the Iran war, while also warning Europe of delays to weapons shipments.
The Pentagon plans to withdraw 5,000 soldiers from Germany in a dispute over the Iran war, as Donald Trump retaliates against Friedrich Merz's criticism of the US-Israeli war effort.
The UAE has departed OPEC as Iran experiences economic strain from a blockade, while Exxon Mobil and Chevron reported strong first-quarter earnings due to higher oil and natural gas prices.
